title: Hidden Field Detection
description: Identify non-display fields that may contain sensitive data or bypass controls.

steps.title: Understanding 3270 Attributes
steps.screen:
3270 Field Attributes:

  Protected/Unprotected - Can user modify?
  Intensity (Normal/Bright/Non-display)
  Modified Data Tag (MDT)
  Numeric/Alpha

Non-display fields are INVISIBLE to user but sent to host.
steps.expected: Understand that 3270 screens can have hidden input fields.
steps.hint: Non-display attribute hides field contents but they still exist and can be modified.
steps.assessment: Q5: 3270 ≠ HTML. Hidden fields work differently than web forms.

steps.title: Password Field Analysis
steps.screen:
TSO LOGON SCREEN (Field Map):

Row 5:  'Userid ===>' [unprotected, normal]
Row 6:  'Password ===>' [unprotected, non-display]
Row 7:  'Account ===>' [unprotected, normal]
Row 10: [protected, non-display] <-- Hidden field!
steps.expected: Identify a hidden field that users cannot see but exists.
steps.hint: Row 10 has a protected non-display field. What data might be stored there?
steps.assessment: Q5: Screen layout can hide data from users but not from inspection.

steps.title: Session Token Hidden Field
steps.screen:
Application Menu (Field Analysis):

Visible: Menu options 1-9
Hidden:  Position (5,60) contains 'SESSID=A7B3C9D2'
         Position (24,1) contains 'PREVMENU=MAIN'

These hidden fields maintain application state.
steps.expected: Recognize that hidden fields often contain session/state data.
steps.hint: Applications use hidden fields for state management. These can be manipulated.
steps.assessment: Q5: Application state in hidden fields - not server-side sessions like web apps.

steps.title: Hidden Field Manipulation
steps.screen:
Original hidden field: AUTHCODE=READER

Modified to: AUTHCODE=ADMIN

[Submit]

Application response: 'Welcome, Administrator'

Authorization bypass via hidden field tampering.
steps.expected: Understand that hidden field manipulation can bypass client-side controls.
steps.hint: If the application trusts hidden field values, tampering can escalate privileges.
steps.assessment: Q5: Trust assumptions - applications may trust 3270 hidden fields.
